The **2018 [World Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/World_Figure_Skating_Championships "World Figure Skating Championships")** were held in [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") from 19–25 March 2018,[[1]](#cite_note-1) at the [Mediolanum Forum](/wiki/Mediolanum_Forum "Mediolanum Forum").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Qualification")]

### Age and minimum TES requirements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Age and minimum TES requirements")]

Skaters are eligible for the 2018 World Championships if they turned 15 years of age before 1 July 2017 and have met the minimum technical elements score requirements. The ISU accepts scores if they were obtained at senior-level ISU-recognized international competitions at least 21 days before the first official practice day of the championships.[[8]](#cite_note-Announce-8)

Minimum technical scores (TES)[[8]](#cite_note-Announce-8)

| --- | --- | --- |
| Men | 34 | 64 |
| Ladies | 27 | 47 |
| Pairs | 25 | 43 |
| Ice dance | 29 | 39 |
| Must be achieved at an ISU-recognized international event   in the ongoing or preceding season.   SP and FS scores may be attained at different events. | | |

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]
